content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all 4 pending amendments for round 18: (1) Added early-exit quorum check to prompt_review.rs exhaustion handler matching the pattern in completion.rs and final_review.rs — after each validator exhaustion, computes effective_min and checks if executed_count + remaining validators can still meet it; if not, breaks out of the loop for the post-loop quorum check to propagate the BackendExhausted error. (2) Extended the 'retry after' duration parser to handle unitless large numbers: when 'retry after 3600' has no unit suffix (rest is empty), the numeric value is parsed and treated as persistent if >= 3600 (1 hour). This uses a higher threshold than the unit-suffixed case (>= 60 for 's'/'sec') because without a unit, the value is ambiguous and only clearly persistent at hour+ scale. Added 3 test assertions for unitless values. (3) Acknowledged the raw stdout fallback gap (intentionally removed in round 17) by adding explicit trade-off documentation at all 3 call sites explaining that plain-text-only stdout errors won't be detected but stderr scanning covers all known backends. The fallback was not restored because the false-positive risk from model conversation output outweighs the false-negative risk. (4) Acknowledged the prompt_review degradation scope extension by adding prominent comments in both prompt_review.rs (Step 3 validator loop) and engine.rs (dispatch_prompt_review_panel) documenting the rationale: prompt-review degradation trades validator coverage for run survivability, and the alternative is aborting the entire run when a single backend exhausts. The degradation was retained rather than rolled back.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Add early-exit quorum check to prompt_review.rs exhaustion handler (line ~230): after incrementing total_exhausted_count and setting last_exhaustion_error, compute effective_min = min_reviewers.min(panel.validators.len().saturating_sub(total_exhausted_count)).max(1) and check if executed_count + remaining validators < effective_min. If quorum is impossible, break; otherwise continue. This matches the pattern in completion.rs and final_review.rs, preventing unnecessary invocations when quorum is already unachievable.
2. [Completed] Extend 'retry after' duration parser in is_backend_exhausted to handle unitless large numbers: when rest is empty (no unit suffix), parse the numeric value and classify as persistent if >= 3600.0 (1 hour). The threshold is higher than the unit-suffixed case (>= 60 for seconds) because unitless values are ambiguous. Unified the check by adding rest.is_empty() to the existing 's'/'sec' condition and using a conditional threshold.
3. [Completed] Add 3 test assertions for unitless retry-after values in detects_rate_limit_with_retry_after_large_seconds test: 'retry after 3600' detected, 'retry after 86400' detected, 'retry after 30' NOT detected (below 3600 threshold).
4. [Completed] Document the raw stdout fallback trade-off at all 3 call sites (process_backend.rs fresh-retry line ~1448, process_backend.rs main failure line ~1490, tmux.rs line ~633): added 'Trade-off: plain-text-only stdout errors won't be detected; stderr scanning covers all known backends' to each comment block. The fallback was not restored — the round 17 removal was intentional and well-reasoned.
5. [Completed] Document the prompt_review degradation scope extension in prompt_review.rs Step 3 (line ~171): added NOTE comment explaining the scope extension trades validator coverage for run survivability, acknowledging that min_reviewers may be effectively reduced when validators exhaust.
6. [Completed] Document the scope extension in engine.rs dispatch_prompt_review_panel (line ~6550): added NOTE comment referencing the rationale in prompt_review.rs Step 3.
7. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to fix formatting (test assertion compaction, .min() chain single-lining). Verified cargo fmt --check passes clean.
8. [Completed] Ran cargo test (754 passed, 0 failed), verified cargo clippy has 0 new warnings in modified files (16 pre-existing in unrelated files), confirmed cargo fmt --check clean.

## Validation Evidence

- All 754 tests pass (cargo test): 572 lib + 166 integration + 1 bench + 754 total, 0 failures, 0 ignored (excluding doc-tests)
- cargo fmt --check passes with no formatting differences
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings produces 0 errors in modified files (prompt_review.rs, process_backend.rs, tmux.rs, engine.rs); all 16 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- prompt_review.rs early-exit quorum check: after each exhaustion, computes effective_min from min_reviewers.min(remaining).max(1) and breaks if quorum impossible, matching completion.rs and final_review.rs patterns
- detects_rate_limit_with_retry_after_large_seconds test passes with 3 new assertions: 'retry after 3600' (unitless, >= 3600) detected, 'retry after 86400' (unitless, >= 3600) detected, 'retry after 30' (unitless, < 3600) NOT detected
- All existing rate-limit tests still pass: 'retry after 3600s' (with unit), 'retry after 86400 seconds' (with unit), 'retry after 60 sec' (with unit) all detected; 'retry after 1s', 'retry after 100ms', 'retry after 30s' (with unit, < 60) all NOT detected
- All existing exhaustion pattern tests pass: usage limit, quota exceeded, credits exhausted, credits depleted, purchase more credits, insufficient_quota, billing hard limit, hard limit reached
- All panel degradation tests pass: completion (degrade + fail), final_review (degrade + fail), prompt_review (degrade + fail)
- Raw stdout trade-off documented at all 3 call sites (process_backend.rs ×2, tmux.rs ×1) with explicit acknowledgment that plain-text-only stdout errors won't be detected
- Prompt-review scope extension documented in prompt_review.rs Step 3 and engine.rs dispatch_prompt_review_panel with rationale
- 4 files changed: prompt_review.rs (early-exit quorum check + scope doc), process_backend.rs (unitless retry-after + 3 test assertions + trade-off docs), tmux.rs (trade-off doc), engine.rs (scope doc)

## Outstanding Risks

- The unitless large-number threshold (>= 3600) is conservative. A backend that says 'retry after 1800' (30 minutes, no unit) would be treated as transient. If real-world backends use unitless values in the 60-3599 range for persistent rate limits, the threshold may need lowering. The threshold can be adjusted without changing the detection logic.
- The raw stdout fallback remains intentionally removed. A backend that prints 'usage limit' as plain text to stdout (no JSON envelope, no stderr output) will not be detected as BackendExhausted. All known supported backends (Claude, Codex, OpenRouter) use either stderr or JSON error envelopes for error reporting. If a new backend is added that uses plain-text stdout for errors, the fallback would need to be reconsidered.
- Prompt-review degradation extends the original feature scope. The configured min_reviewers can be effectively reduced when validators exhaust, meaning a refined prompt may be accepted with fewer validators than intended. Operators who rely on the validator count as a strict safety gate should be aware that exhaustion can lower the effective quorum. If strict validation is required regardless of backend availability, the degradation logic in prompt_review.rs would need a configuration flag to opt out.
- The prompt_review early-exit quorum check sets last_exhaustion_error before computing effective_min. If a future refactoring moves the error assignment after the break check, the error would be lost on break. The current ordering is correct (error assigned, then break-or-continue decision).
